description: WCAG 2+ extension layer: automated-or-manual rules for creative, civic, educational, and multimodal interfaces that go beyond conventional HTML audits.

rules.title: Canvas needs an accessible description
rules.description: Canvas-based sketches need text descriptions so nonvisual users can understand the sketch.
nextSteps: Add p5 describe() for the overall canvas.
Use describeElement() for important objects or interactions.
Use textOutput() or gridOutput() when shape descriptions are useful.

rules.title: Mouse/touch-only interaction needs a keyboard alternative
rules.description: Interactions driven only by mousePressed, mouseDragged, mouseMoved, or touchStarted are unusable without a pointing device.
nextSteps: Add keyPressed() handling or visible button controls that trigger the same behavior.
Ensure focus order and focus indication are clear for any added controls.

rules.title: Continuous animation needs a pause or reduce-motion control
rules.description: Sketches with a running draw() loop that animates continuously can be disorienting or triggering without a way to pause or reduce motion.
nextSteps: Avoid motion that is not essential to the sketch's meaning.

rules.title: PDF is not tagged for accessibility
rules.description: This PDF has no tag structure (no /MarkInfo Marked entry). Without tags, screen readers cannot reliably determine reading order, headings, lists, tables, or alternative text, and PDF/UA conformance is not possible.
nextSteps: Re-export the PDF from an authoring tool (Word, InDesign, Acrobat) with tagging enabled, or run it through Acrobat's 'Autotag Document' as a starting point.
Review and correct the generated tag tree, since autotagging is not fully reliable on its own.

rules.title: PDF has no specified document language
rules.description: The PDF catalog has no /Lang entry, so screen readers cannot determine which language to use for pronunciation and voice selection.
nextSteps: Set the document language in the authoring tool's document properties, or in Acrobat under File > Properties > Advanced.
If sections of the document use a different language than the rest, tag those sections with their own language attribute.

rules.title: PDF has no document title
rules.description: The PDF's document properties have no Title set, so screen readers and browser tabs fall back to the file name, which is often unclear.
nextSteps: Set a clear, descriptive document title in the authoring tool's document properties or Acrobat's Description tab.
Also set the viewer preference to display the document title instead of the file name, where supported.
